[[File:Soulreaper axe detail.png|left|150px]]
The '''Soulreaper axe''' is a two-handed axe that requires 80 [[Attack]] and [[Strength]] to wield. It is created by combining four untradeable components, obtained as drops from [[The Leviathan]], [[The Whisperer]], [[Vardorvis]], and [[Duke Sucellus]], alongside 2,000 [[blood rune]]s and level 75 [[Magic]] (which cannot be boosted).

Despite being made from items dropped by prominent [[Zarosians]], the item itself '''does not''' grant [[god protection]] towards Zarosian NPCs in the [[God Wars Dungeon]].

==Soul stacks==
[[File:Soul Stack.png|left]]
''Soul stacks'' are generated every time a player makes an attack and has not reached five stacks; they will also take 8 damage, even if that attack was not a [[successful hit]]. Each stack is added after the damage from the player's attack is calculated; so an attack does not benefit from the 6% bonus it just generated. 

Each ''Soul stack'' provides a 6% boost to a player's [[Strength]] Level, up to a maximum of +30% at five stacks. This boost is additive with, and works similarly to the Strength multipliers from prayers like [[Piety]] or [[Ultimate Strength]].  ''Soul stacks'' do not affect other weapons. 

When the player can maintain five stacks for the 30% strength bonus, the soulreaper axe has the capability to deal more damage than weapons like the [[inquisitor's mace]] or [[blade of Saeldor]], and can even out-DPS the [[scythe of vitur]] against 1x1 or 2x2-sized monsters. Against 3x3 or larger sized monsters, the scythe of vitur will typically provide a higher damage output. The boost provided from the soul stacks can be further increased with boosts such as [[Super strength]] potions and strength-boosting gear.

''Soul stacks'' are maintained as long as a player is actively attacking with the Soulreaper axe equipped. In areas with aggressive enemies, very little input is required to keep the strength buff maximised. This makes the axe exceptional for combat encounters with little downtime, including bosses like [[Araxxor]] and [[Vardorvis]], and lower intensity combat like [[Slayer]] and the [[Nightmare Zone]].

===Health management===
Players using the Soulreaper axe can expect to lose 40 [[Hitpoints]] over 15 seconds when starting a fresh fight with zero ''Soul stacks''. The damage each stack deals can be healed or restored immediately and should be carefully taken into account when fighting dangerous enemies. Players do not take any additional damage from attacking if they already have the max of five ''Soul stacks.'' 

If leaving combat with five ''Soul stacks'' a player will be healed 8 Hitpoints every 20 ticks (12 seconds) for a total of 40 Hitpoints (over 60 seconds). Because ''Soul stacks'' do not overheal, 8 hitpoints of healing is lost if a player is at full health and a stack is used or expires, so players should be mindful of how much they need to heal if a stack will expire. In fights where the player is unable to make an attack every 12 seconds, this can result in wasted food and unnecessary healing.

==Special attack==
[[File:Behead.gif|thumb|250px|A player performing the soulreaper axe's special attack, ''Behead,'' with five Soul Stacks.]]
The Soulreaper axe has a [[special attack]], ''Behead'', which consumes all Soul Stacks and does not require special attack energy.

For each Soul Stack consumed, ''Behead'' increases the player's accuracy and damage by 6% and heals them by 8 Hitpoints, effectively restoring any health spent to create those Soul stacks. Players are healed regardless of whether the attack was a [[successful hit]] or not. If a player is at full health and has active ''Soul stacks'' they can utilise them as a temporary health bank; using ''Behead'' to restore up to 40 hitpoints instantly when needed.

Equipping the Soulreaper axe replaces the special attack interface with a unique graphic ([[File:Soul Stack.png|link=]]) that indicates the Soul stack resource, however player's special attack energy continues to regenerate in the background normally. Since the special attack only consumes Soul stacks, In PvP it's useful to combo ''Behead'' into special attacks from other weapons like the [[granite maul]], [[Dragon claws]] or [[Voidwaker]] for high amounts of burst damage on the target and burst healing for yourself.

===Mechanics===
Although the in-game description states ''Behead'' deals additional damage, this is in relation to base damage and not the boosted level from active soul stacks.

When using ''Behead'', the [[Strength]] boost from Soul stacks is consumed first and a new separate ''damage'' bonus is applied to the swing.  Although there can be slight differences because of the order of operations, ''Behead'' typically deals a similar maximum hit as a player that does nothing and continues to auto attack ''(typically within 0-3 points of damage)''. Consequently the strength bonus is then lost, meaning ''Behead'' also costs 90% of an additional attack when a player continues combat.

Note: in rare situations, the alternate damage calculation of the special attack can result in ''Behead'' having a lower max hit than a regular auto attack.

The accuracy roll for ''Behead'' uses the attack bonus of the player's currently selected style, which is rolled against the corresponding defensive bonuses of an opponent – either slash or crush in this case.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1699335106479968443|date=6 September 2023|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/yKd5K{{^|https://archive.ph/o3xU9}}|archivedate=6 September 2023|quote=[Could you look into the soul reaper axe? I submitted a bug report already. Im using it in pvp and there is 0 chance the accuary buff  is working on the spec. (...)] Looks like it's applying it on the spec, nevertheless, rolling the result against the target's slash or crush defence depending on your active style (which is unusual for a spec).}} This is in contrast to most other special attacks, which roll against the defensive bonuses of an opponent of a specific combat style regardless of the attacking combat style chosen (for example, the [[dragon dagger]]'s special attack will always roll against the opponent's slash defence, even if the attack style is set to stab).

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 7 January 2026
|update = QoL Tweaks & Fixes
|change = The soulreaper axe's [[special attack]] will no longer heal the player if they have zero [[hitpoints]].
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 4 September 2024
|update = Araxxor Tweaks & Poll 82 Updates
|change = Soul Stacks will now decrement if the player deposits the axe into the bank and then logs out.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 28 August 2024
|update = Araxxor
|change = Soul Stacks no longer disappear when the player teleports or is subject to a teleportation effect.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 7 August 2024
|update = Poll 82 Changes
|change = 
* The running animation while holding the soulreaper axe was updated.
* The special attack animation has been adjusted to render properly when viewed at various angles outside of the player's tile.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 5 June 2024
|update = Pride 2024
|type = hotfix
|change = A bug has been fixed that was causing lost soul stacks to lower the max hit of other weapons.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 29 May 2024
|update = Project Rebalance: Combat Changes
|change = * Soul Stacks no longer disappear upon switching weapons.
* The duration in which players can stop attacking without losing Soul Stacks has been doubled.
* Soul Stacks lost from not attacking anything will now heal the player rather than disappearing completely.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 10 January 2024
|update = Children of the Sun
|change = The soulreaper axe's non-special attack animations no longer stalls the attacker's movement.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 15 November 2023
|update = Leagues IV - Trailblazer Reloaded
|change = The soulreaper axe now sends a [[Chat Interface#Filters|filterable]] message to alert players that Soul Stacks are lost when the axe is unequipped.
}}
{{Subject changes
|update = Desert Treasure II - Further Changes
|date = 9 August 2023
|change = * The soulreaper axe will no longer prevent a player from using its special attack if they do not have enough regular [[Special_attacks#Energy|special attack energy]].
* If a [[Phoenix necklace]]'s healing effect procs, it will no longer reset Soul Stacks.
* Players can no longer pull up an interface to stall the Soul Stack timer.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 30 July 2023
|type = hotfix
|change = An issue with the special attack that caused it to deal extremely high amounts of damage (up to 529) has been fixed.
}}
{{Subject changes
|prerelease = yes
|page = Soulreaper axe (beta)
|daterange = 14 - 21 June 2023
|beta = Ruinous Powers Beta v2
|change = 
* The axe's strength bonus has been increased from +115 to +121.
* The amount of time that passes before Mighty Stacks are lost has been increased from 8 ticks (4.8 seconds) to 10 ticks (6 seconds).
}}
{{Subject changes
|daterange = 12 - 18 April 2023
|beta = Desert Treasure II Rewards Beta
|change = First [[Soulreaper axe (beta)|present]] in the Desert Treasure II Rewards beta.

==Trivia==
* The soulreaper axe was based on a suggestion by Reddit user ''moronijess'',{{CiteForum|author = moronijess|url = https://www.reddit.com/r/2007scape/comments/117fp21/sandbreaker_axe_concept_for_community_design/|title = Sandbreaker Axe Concept for Community Design|forum = Reddit|postdate = 20 February 2023}} who was credited for the suggestion in an update newspost.{{CiteNews|url=https://secure.runescape.com/m=news/desert-treasure-ii-rewards-beta?oldschool=1|newsdate=12 April 2023|author=The Old School Team|title=Desert Treasure II Rewards Beta}}
* Despite the poll blog and newspost stating the axe requires full completion of [[Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire]] to wield, this is not the case in-game.
